name: github-triage
tagline: Read-only GitHub issue/PR triage orchestrator that spawns background subagents to write evidence-backed, permalinked report files.
verdict: Strong no-mutation design repeated at multiple layers and safe to re-run. Main risk: raw issue/PR body text is pasted straight into subagent prompts, an indirect-prompt-injection vector guarded only by prose rules, not tool-level enforcement. It also leans on non-standard orchestration primitives (task_create/background_output) tied to its own framework, so

highlights.summary: Issue/PR `body` and `comments_summary` (untrusted, author-controlled text) are interpolated verbatim into each subagent's prompt (lines 231-232, 274-276, etc.), defended only by prose 'NEVER run gh is
No negative-trigger guidance anywhere (e.g. private/no-gh-auth repos, empty backlog, cases where real GitHub actions are actually wanted).

No guidance for gh/API failures, rate limits, auth errors, zero-open-items repos, or a subagent that errors/times out mid-analysis; Phase 4 only covers the happy path of polling completed background t
